text.tspan: Buying what is only mission critical and deferring non-critical upgrades
Rightsizing my memory needs instead of "over provisioning"
Pulling forward purchases of key hardware to get ahead of price increase
Increasing our hardware budgets outright
Revisiting our entire on-premise hardware strategy / considering a more permanent shift of
workloads to the cloud
Renegotiating vendor contracts / increasing usage of LTAs
Accelerating adoption of consumption-based hardware programs (DELL APEX, HPE
GreenLake, NTAP Keystone, PSTG Evergreen//One, etc)
Delaying purchases in anticipation of future vendor concessions
Shifting more workloads from the cloud to on-premise data centers
Leaning into software and creative configurations to increase memory efficiency
Refreshing our CPUs; adding memory later when pricing normalizes
Reducing our hardware budgets outright
Temporarily shifting certain workloads to the cloud
Leaning into our on-premise data center strategy
Buying de-spec'ed products
It's too early to determine any actions to take / not sure
Memory prices aren't influencing my purchasing habits/patterns
Actions Taken Given the Risk of Significant Price Increases Caused by Memory Price Inflation and Risk of Insufficient Memory

ply (1Q26
Survey)
